title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown · Type-System Trace: Feed Ts Postgres Error Policy
summary: Each request creates a pool, transaction statements call pool.query, checked-out clients leak on error, row values are trusted, and process exit races pool timers. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Narrow unknown errors into constraint, serialization, deadlock, timeout, cancel, connection, and unknown causes without message matching or leakage.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Postgres Error Policy model · Theory Feed Ts Pos…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Postgres Error Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Postgres Error Policy model · Theory Feed Ts Pos…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2115 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Pool, checked-out client, transaction connection, row validation, error, and close ownership; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Narrow unknown errors into constraint, serialization, deadlock, timeout, cancel, connection, and unknown causes without message matching or leakage.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Postgres Error Policy model · Theory Feed Ts Pos…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: All pg errors are safe Error instances with stable text.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: feed-ts-postgres-error-policy-1fjvh2.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-ts-postgres-error-policy-1fjvh2(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_feed_ts_postgres_error_policy_1fjvh2\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?databaseCases < 10)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?releasedClients < 8 \|\| releasedClients > databaseCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?transactionCases < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leakedClients > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
